text: The surgeon demonstrates the use of the Maryland dissector, manipulating it with wrist movements to orient the instrument at various angles for precise dissection.
The surgeon uses a bipolar energy device to coagulate small vessels, minimizing heat displacement compared to monopolar devices.
The surgeon isolates and ligates the inferior pulmonary vein first due to the incomplete fissure in the right lower lobectomy procedure.
The surgeon at the bedside demonstrates the ease of changing instruments, switching between a sucker and a standard diathermy hook without relying on an assistant.

The surgeon precisely controls the Maryland dissector to follow the tissue planes around the anterior hilum, while using fenestrated forceps in the other hand to perform blunt dissection around the vessel.

The surgeon uses fenestrated forceps to carefully dissect around the inferior pulmonary vein, preparing the path for stapling. The instruments provide tactile feedback, enhancing the efficiency and safety of the procedure.
The surgeon introduces the stapler through a subxiphoid port, preparing to staple the pulmonary structures without relying on an assistant.
